title: Science Synthesis Chamber Command Grammar
purpose: Define the command surface for structured scientific intake, comparison, synthesis, translation, and civic relevance workflows.

command_philosophy.principle: Science commands should convert curiosity into disciplined, reviewable, provenance-aware workflows.
design_rules: separate intake, comparison, translation, and civic implication into distinct commands
preserve provenance and uncertainty at command time
support both quick scans and deep multi-cycle research flows
do not allow translation commands to bypass scientific structure

command_families.description: Bring scientific sources, datasets, or claims into the chamber with proper structure.

commands.name: /science-intake
commands.intent: Register a scientific source, dataset, or study for chamber analysis.

commands.name: /dataset-review
commands.intent: Inspect a scientific dataset for schema, provenance, scope, and limits.

commands.name: /source-provenance
commands.intent: Generate or inspect provenance details for a scientific source.

command_families.description: Classify, compare, and analyze scientific findings.

commands.name: /science-scan
commands.intent: Perform a first-pass structured scan of a scientific question or source set.

commands.name: /compare-findings
commands.intent: Compare multiple studies or datasets addressing a related scientific question.

commands.name: /consensus-map
commands.intent: Map consensus, emerging signal, frontier, dispute, or insufficient evidence across sources.

commands.name: /method-check
commands.intent: Classify the method type and identify methodological limits or mismatch.

commands.name: /confidence-score
commands.intent: Estimate confidence using provenance, method, replication, and cross-source consistency.

command_families.description: Translate validated science into human-readable, meaning-aware output without losing rigor.

commands.name: /jade-translate-science
commands.intent: Translate scientifically structured findings into clear human language.

commands.name: /why-it-matters
commands.intent: Explain the human significance of a scientific finding.

commands.name: /science-brief
commands.intent: Produce a concise structured brief suitable for operators or readers.

command_families.description: Map science into public relevance, institutions, resilience, and policy caution.

commands.name: /civic-science-brief
commands.intent: Show how a scientific finding matters for society, resilience, infrastructure, or public institutions.

commands.name: /policy-caution
commands.intent: Identify where scientific evidence is too narrow or uncertain for strong policy claims.

commands.name: /resilience-relevance
commands.intent: Extract resilience, preparedness, or infrastructure relevance from scientific evidence.

command_families.description: Track frontier science and changes over time without overclaiming maturity.

commands.name: /frontier-watch
commands.intent: Track an emerging scientific area that is promising but not settled.

commands.name: /what-changed
commands.intent: Identify what meaningfully changed in a scientific domain relative to prior chamber state.

commands.name: /research-watch
commands.intent: Create a monitoring brief for frontier findings worth revisiting later.

command_families.description: Preserve canon, lineage, and chamber continuity for scientific knowledge.

commands.name: /science-ledger
commands.intent: Create, inspect, or append a scientific ledger record.

commands.name: /archive-consensus
commands.intent: Write a stabilized consensus entry into the science archive.

commands.name: /reopen-chamber
commands.intent: Reopen an archived or watching science chamber when meaningful new evidence appears.
